issues.main_description_html:
Description

Remove the Manual Blur page from the tour flow.

This is the page reached at route my-tours/:mid/blur/manual (e.g. app.gothru.co/my-tours/3537090/blur/manual), under Anonymize 360 Images → Manual Blur. It currently presents two options:

  • Auto Selection Blur Area ("Auto Selection" button)
  • Blur Selected Area ("Blur Selected" button)

This page should be removed.

Scope

  • Remove the Manual Blur page/route (my-tours/:mid/blur/manual).
  • Remove any navigation/links that lead to this page (e.g. the "Manual Blur" entry point from the Anonymize 360 Images section).
  • Ensure removal does not break related blur/anonymize flows or leave dead routes.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The Manual Blur page (my-tours/:mid/blur/manual) is no longer accessible from the UI.
  2. No navigation element or button links to the removed page.
  3. Visiting the old route directly does not produce a broken/blank state (redirects or 404s gracefully).

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Tour editor: face/plate blurring). Related to #4073 (Blur Section UI Refinements & AI Feature Cleanup).

Demo from Development

issues.display_title: Issue #4076: Add Tutorial Link & Update Tooltip on "Map Rejected" Icon
issues.main_description_html:
Description

To better assist users when their image receives a "Map Rejected" status, we need to provide immediate, actionable help directly from the image grid UI.

Currently, hovering over the rejection indicator (the purple exclamation mark icon) only states the status. We need to update the tooltip text to encourage users to click, and make the icon clickable so it redirects them to a tutorial video on how to resolve the rejection.

Sub-tasks

  • Update Tooltip Text: Modify the existing hover tooltip on the purple exclamation mark icon.
    • New Text: "Map Rejected - Click to see how to fix" (or append the click instruction to the existing text).
  • Implement Click Action: Add an onClick event or wrap the icon in an anchor tag. Ensure the CSS cursor changes to pointer on hover so users know it is interactive.
  • External Redirect: Configure the click action to open the following YouTube tutorial video in a new browser tab (using target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer"):
    • Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAS79cuZaWw

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Hovering over the purple rejection icon displays the newly updated tooltip text.
  2. The icon acts as a clickable button (shows a pointer cursor on hover).
  3. Clicking the icon successfully opens the correct YouTube video in a new browser tab without closing the current application state.

Test Scenario (for Tester / QA)

Preconditions

  • Logged in and viewing a tour whose 360 image grid contains at least one image with a "Map Rejected" status (purple exclamation mark icon on the thumbnail card).

Scenario 1 — Tooltip text

  1. Hover the cursor over the purple "!" rejection icon.
  2. ✅ Expected: Tooltip shows the updated text — "Map Rejected - Click to see how to fix" (or the existing status text with the click instruction appended).

Scenario 2 — Interactive cursor

  1. Hover over the purple "!" icon.
  2. ✅ Expected: The cursor changes to a pointer (hand), signalling the icon is clickable.

Scenario 3 — Click redirect (happy path)

  1. Click the purple "!" icon.
  2. ✅ Expected: A new browser tab opens to https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iAS79cuZaWw.
  3. ✅ Expected: The original GoThru tab/application state is unchanged (not navigated away, no reload, current page/selection preserved).

Scenario 4 — Security attributes

  1. Inspect the icon's link/handler (DevTools).
  2. ✅ Expected: Opens via target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" (new tab cannot access window.opener).

Scenario 5 — Regression / non-rejected images

  1. Hover/click on images that are not "Map Rejected" (e.g. green/approved status).
  2. ✅ Expected: No tutorial link or rejection tooltip appears; their behavior is unchanged.

Pass criteria: All scenarios behave as the ✅ Expected results; the YouTube link is correct and opens safely in a new tab without disrupting the app.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §2.2 / §4 (Tour editor & publishing status). The purple "!" icon appears on image thumbnail cards in the 360 image grid.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-17.14-17-50.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4077: Add Bottom Pagination Controls to Google Portfolio Grid
issues.main_description_html:
Description

A UX friction point has been identified on the "Google Portfolio" page. Currently, the pagination controls (page numbers to navigate to the next/previous page) are only located at the top of the tour grid.

When a user changes their view settings to display a large number of tours per page (e.g., 24 or 36 items), they scroll down to view them. Once they reach the bottom of the list, they are forced to scroll all the way back to the top just to click the "Next Page" button.

To improve navigation flow, we need to duplicate the existing top pagination component and place it at the bottom of the grid as well.

Sub-tasks

  • Component Duplication: Re-use the existing pagination component that currently sits at the top of the list.
  • UI Placement: Render a second instance of this pagination component at the very bottom of the tour grid container (below the last row of tour cards).
  • State Synchronization: Ensure that both the top and bottom pagination components share the same state. Clicking "Page 2" on the bottom component must correctly update the list and reflect the active state on both paginators.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Pagination numbers/controls are clearly visible at both the top and the bottom of the Google Portfolio tour grid.
  2. Users can successfully navigate to different pages using the new bottom pagination controls.
  3. The UI layout remains clean and the new bottom paginator does not overlap with the chat widget or footer elements.

Test Scenario (for Tester / QA)

Preconditions

  • Logged in to an account that has enough Google Portfolio tours to produce multiple pages at higher page sizes (24 / 36 per page).
  • Open the Google Portfolio page.

Scenario 1 — Bottom paginator visible

  1. Set "tours per page" to 24 (then repeat with 36).
  2. Scroll to the bottom of the tour grid.
  3. ✅ Expected: A pagination control (same as the top one) is visible at the bottom, below the last row of tour cards.

Scenario 2 — Navigate using bottom controls

  1. At the bottom paginator, click "Next Page" / "Page 2".
  2. ✅ Expected: The grid loads the correct next page of tours.

Scenario 3 — State synchronization (both paginators in sync)

  1. Click Page 2 on the bottom paginator.
  2. Scroll to the top.
  3. ✅ Expected: The top paginator also shows Page 2 as active. (And vice versa: changing the page from the top updates the bottom.)

Scenario 4 — Consistency across page sizes

  1. Change page size (12 → 24 → 36) and navigate using the bottom controls each time.
  2. ✅ Expected: Both paginators always reflect the same current page and the correct total page count for the selected page size.

Scenario 5 — Layout / no overlap

  1. With the bottom paginator showing, inspect the area near the bottom-right chat widget and the footer (Terms / Privacy / © GoThru).
  2. ✅ Expected: The bottom paginator does not overlap or get hidden behind the chat widget or footer; layout stays clean on desktop and at smaller/responsive widths.

Scenario 6 — Single page edge case

  1. Set a page size large enough that all tours fit on one page.
  2. ✅ Expected: Pagination behaves sensibly (no broken/duplicate controls; consistent with the top paginator's behavior).

Pass criteria: All scenarios behave as the ✅ Expected results, top and bottom paginators stay perfectly in sync, and the new control never overlaps the chat widget or footer.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Portfolio & custom filters).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-17.13-10-55.mp4
